results.title: Agentic AI: The Fix for Broken Workflows That Slow Your Business Down
results.content:
Agentic AI shifts this model completely. It operates proactively. It anticipates needs, identifies patterns, and acts before problems escalate. This allows businesses to move from constantly fixing issues to staying ahead of them. Alpharive’s approach focuses on building systems that don’t just react but continuously improve how work gets done.

### The Role of Agentic AI in Scaling Without Chaos

Scaling a business often introduces complexity. More customers, more processes, and more dependencies usually lead to more friction. Traditional systems struggle to handle this increase because they require more manual coordination as they grow. [...] Agentic AI is not just another layer of automation. It is designed to act with intent. Instead of waiting for instructions, it understands goals, evaluates context, and takes actions independently to achieve outcomes. This shift from task execution to goal-driven behavior is what makes it fundamentally different.

An Agentic AI system can monitor workflows continuously, identify bottlenecks as they happen, and take corrective actions without waiting for human input. At Alpharive, we build AI agents that align with business objectives, ensuring that processes are not just automated but actively optimized as they run.

### How Broken Workflows Impact Growth More Than You Think [...] Agentic AI allows businesses to scale without adding operational chaos. Since it can manage workflows dynamically, it reduces the need for constant supervision and intervention. At Alpharive, we ensure that AI agents are built with scalability in mind, so businesses can grow without compromising efficiency or control.

### Why Businesses That Adopt Early Will Win Faster

Every major shift in technology creates a gap between early adopters and late adopters. The same is happening with Agentic AI. Businesses that integrate it early are not just improving efficiency. They are redefining how work gets done.

results.title: Agentic AI
results.content:
### Decision bias

Let's talk about bias first. Agentic AI might focus on fixing problems on your most-visited pages because that impacts the most users. This sounds logical but could result in accessibility issues on less-popular pages staying broken longer. Or a marketing AI that chases engagement numbers might start pushing flashy content over useful information.

### Privacy concerns

Privacy adds another wrinkle. There are instances in which the function of a program impacts user privacy by design. For example:

While no AI solution handles compliance completely on its own, good platforms help by limiting what they collect. They also give you clear insight into what information they're gathering about your users.

### Retention of control [...] ### Content management

Modern agentic AI content systems spot broken links, fix the simple ones, and only bother you with tricky problems that need your judgment. They watch how people use your content and suggest updates based on actual user behavior.

### Digital accessibility

Smart automation has replaced manually running accessibility scans and sorting through endless reports. Agentic AI systems fix common issues automatically, help writers create accessible content from the start, and prioritize fixes based on real user impact. When someone using a screen reader struggles with a page, the system notices and bumps that fix to the top of the list.

### Marketing automation [...] Understanding how agentic AI works (and where human intervention must remain) matters whether you work with digital tools, manage user experiences, or just care about the choices your software makes when you're not looking.

## On this page

## Agentic AI vs. Non-agentic AI

Agentic AI focuses on achieving outcomes rather than executing commands. When monitoring website performance, it identifies unusual patterns and intervenes before small issues become problems.

Non-agentic AI works like a tool, not a teammate. It performs specific tasks, like scanning for broken links or checking readability scores, but can never make judgment calls or shift priorities when conditions change.

Comparison of non-agentic and agentic AI across common tasks

results.title: Agentic Commerce: How AI Is Changing Online Shopping Behavior | Clutch.co
results.content:
Looking ahead, 32% said they would use an AI assistant for holiday shopping in the future. Another 42% indicated they may use it in the future, suggesting that adoption is likely to grow as comfort and familiarity increase.

Image 5: Agentic Commerce: How AI Is Changing Online Shopping Behavior

## Trust, Control, and Consumer Confidence in Agentic Commerce

Despite growing use, trust remains a significant barrier to the deeper adoption of AI in the shopping journey. Overall, 91% of consumers report concerns or frustrations when using AI assistants for shopping.

Image 6: Agentic Commerce: How AI Is Changing Online Shopping Behavior

Data and security are top worries, cited by 43% of respondents.

Additionally, 38% are wary of inaccurate or unreliable product recommendations. [...] ### What Builds (and Breaks) Trust in AI Product Recommendations

Breaking down consumer trust in AI recommendations for specific products further:

   17% generally trust AI-recommended products
   23% trust them only after they follow up with their own research
   26% say they sometimes trust them, and it depends on the product
   17% are skeptical
   Another 17% don’t trust them at all

The distrust for AI-recommended products mainly boils down to a few top concerns and frustrations, with 42% of respondents sharing that suggestions don’t match their taste or style, 38% receiving inaccurate or unreliable recommendations, and 34% skeptical about the AI system’s potential bias toward certain brands or products.

results.title: Agentic Commerce's biggest hurdle is a human problem... yes, it's often us....
results.content:
One of the biggest issues with many of the AI platforms is attribution is weak at best so it’s hard for business to invest in making their website platforms more accessible if they can’t prove what revenue is being driven. Ignoring AI agents for now if you look at your Google Analytics data you can see you got a referral visit from ChatGPT which is great but it doesn’t show you all the activity.

What is missing for attribution is

 the activity within ChatGPT that drove the click (prompt)
 the activity that occurred within ChatGPT before the click (other prompts)
 the activity from those people that didn’t click (saw negative story)
 the activity those who visited a competitor (clicked for a discount coupon) [...] A number of the world’s leading payment providers and banks have spent the last decade increasing their fraud protection tools and 2FA features such as in-app approvals, sms or voice confirmation. Sure some AI agents could clone your voice, read your phone messages or even open the banking app for approval but this may break your terms of service with your bank or card provider.

An early safeguard with AI agents was to provide them with a virtual or pre-paid card but more and more platforms have stopped virtual cards from being used as they don’t support recurring payments, cannot do authorisation holds or can be used for fraud. NS is one of likely a growing number of transport providers that are being taken for a ride as commuters disable the virtual card before the fare is debited. [...] Remember that Agentic commerce may suffer from hallucinations if data isn’t made available. Also, research is showing that AI agents are ignoring an increasing number of human instructions and going as far as publishing/sharing confidential details of their human users such as passwords. The issue with AI agents is that early users are guinea pigs/crash test dummies with little oversight, monitoring or guardrails in place to protect the humans who are utilising them. Furthermore, most publishers, retailers and brands are not even close to being ready for the oncoming flood of AI agent traffic that breaks all existing monetisation, terms & service, fraud protection and attribution models. This article ignores all the potential security issues facing AI agents such as prompt injection that

results.content:
### The Fan Experience Just Got Some New Skills

The AI bottleneck: why your service model is broken (and how to fix it)
The AI bottleneck: why your service model is broken (and how to fix it)

### The AI bottleneck: why your service model is broken (and how to fix it)

Meet the Zendesk Resolution Platform for autonomous service
Meet the Zendesk Resolution Platform for autonomous service

### Meet the Zendesk Resolution Platform for autonomous service

Discover the Zendesk Resolution Platform, where AI agents, AI Copilot, and workflows join together to deliver autonomous service at scale.

New innovations for employee service and ITSM
New innovations for employee service and ITSM

### New innovations for employee service and ITSM

## Turn every conversation into resolution [...] These businesses aren’t just adopting AI, they’re using it to reduce costs, deliver better customer outcomes, and grow faster. And they’re setting an incredibly high bar for everyone else: 70% of customers say they’re noticing a clear difference between companies that use AI well, and those that don’t.

### What makes agentic AI different

At the core of Zendesk AI agents is something new: agentic AI. It’s a different kind of intelligence built to resolve real customer issues, not just react to them. [...] That’s why Zendesk built The Resolution Platform: the only AI-powered system where automation, AI, and agents work together to resolve customer issues from start to finish. At its core, agentic AI gives our AI agents the ability to solve problems on their own—no scripts or training required. They adapt on the fly, collaborate with human agents when needed, draw from shared knowledge, and take action across your systems to ensure every customer gets the right outcome.

### Real businesses, real results

This is already happening.

Zendesk is the largest global provider of agentic AI for service, helping more than 10,000 companies worldwide, resolving up to 80–90% of customer conversations, and powering more than 4.6 billion resolutions each year.
